Home / Revenue Projector

Revenue Projector

Project your MRR growth over 6, 12, or 24 months. Compare different growth rate scenarios to plan your financial future.

How to Use This Tool

1
Enter your current Monthly Recurring Revenue (MRR).
2
Enter your average monthly growth rate as a percentage.
3
Select your projection period (6, 12, or 24 months).
4
Review the month-by-month projection.
5
Scroll down to compare 9 different growth rate scenarios.
6
tools.solopreneur-revenue-projector.how_to_use.5

Frequently Asked Questions

What is a good monthly growth rate for a SaaS?

5-10% monthly growth is considered good for early-stage SaaS. 2-3% is healthy for mature products. Anything above 10% is exceptional.

How do I increase my monthly growth rate?

Focus on three levers: increase traffic (SEO, content, ads), improve conversion rate (better landing page, free trial), and reduce churn (onboarding, customer success).

Is linear or exponential growth more realistic?

SaaS growth is typically exponential in the early stages and linear as you saturate your market. This calculator models exponential growth, which fits most early-stage products.

What if my growth rate varies month to month?

Use your 3-month average. If you had 8%, 5%, and 3%, your average is ~5.3%. Conservative estimates are better for planning.

When should I expect growth to slow?

Growth naturally slows as you capture your initial market. At $10K MRR, 5% is great. At $100K MRR, 2-3% is healthy. The law of large numbers applies.

Results

Copy All
#1 📈 Revenue Projection (12 months at 5% monthly growth) Month 0 (Now): $2,000/mo Month 1: $2,100/mo Month 2: $2,205/mo Month 3: $2,315/mo ← Quarter 1 Month 4: $2,431/mo Month 5: $2,553/mo Month 6: $2,680/mo ← Quarter 2 Month 7: $2,814/mo Month 8: $2,955/mo Month 9: $3,103/mo ← Quarter 3 Month 10: $3,258/mo Month 11: $3,421/mo Month 12: $3,592/mo ← Quarter 4 ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ 📊 Summary: • Starting MRR: $2,000 • Ending MRR: $3,592 • Total Revenue: $30,397 • Growth Multiple: 1.8x 🚀 Strong growth. Focus on sustaining this rate — churn is the biggest threat to compounding. 💡 Compound growth is powerful: a 5% monthly improvement compounds to 80% annual growth. Small wins matter.
#2 Conservative (1%/mo): $2,000 → $2,254 in 12 months (1%/mo)
#3 Moderate (3%/mo): $2,000 → $2,852 in 12 months (3%/mo)
#4 Good (5%/mo): $2,000 → $3,592 in 12 months (5%/mo)
#5 Strong (8%/mo): $2,000 → $5,036 in 12 months (8%/mo)
